success_surface: Successful multi-step data extraction and population with proper cleanup and verification
effective_behaviors: Collect filtered data into memory buffer before writing to target range
Explicitly clear stale data beyond populated range after writing
Log success metrics including count and final range for validation
Use iterative row/column access pattern for precise cell-level operations
Separate source data loading from target writing phases
Preserve original workbook by loading once and saving to distinct output path
evidence: Collected lowest performing students into list first (lowest_performing = []) before writing
Cleared cells beyond populated range: 'for row_num in range(start_row + len(lowest_performing), 69)'
Printed success message with count and range: 'Successfully populated 16... Populated range: B3:C18'
Used cell() method for precise positioning rather than bulk operations
Loaded Data worksheet, wrote to Summary worksheet (separate phases)
Saved to output path different from source file path

memory_items.title: Buffer Before Write Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Always collect filtered/processed data into an in-memory structure before writing to destination ranges
memory_items.generalization: Prevents partial writes and allows counting/validation before committing changes

memory_items.title: Stale Data Cleanup
memory_items.lesson: After writing variable-length result sets, explicitly clear cells beyond the last populated row/column
memory_items.generalization: Ensures no orphaned data from previous executions remains in unused portions of the target range

memory_items.title: Execution Metrics Logging
memory_items.lesson: Report row counts, affected ranges, and success status after completion
memory_items.generalization: Enables downstream verification and troubleshooting without inspecting the file directly

memory_items.title: Two-Phase Processing
memory_items.lesson: Separate data reading/filtering phase from data writing phase
memory_items.generalization: Reduces error risk and makes debugging easier by isolating transformation logic from output logic

edits.content:
## 6. Extract & Populate Best Practices
- **Buffer First**: Collect all filtered/processed records into memory before writing to destination sheets. This enables pre-validation and prevents partial writes.
- **Clean Stale Data**: When writing variable-length results, always clear cells beyond the last populated position to remove orphaned data from previous runs.
- **Log Metrics**: Report the number of rows affected and the final output range (start/end cells) after each population operation.
- **Phase Separation**: Keep data extraction logic separate from data writing logic for cleaner debugging and verification.
